detection_strategy: Perform inter-procedural data-flow analysis. 1) Identify all `ThreadLocal.set()` calls. 2) Trace forward to find if the enclosing method or any caller has a `try-finally` block that guarantees a `ThreadLocal.remove()` call. 3) Flag any `set()` without a corresponding `remove()` in a `finally` block as a potential leak. 4) Additionally, detect `ThreadLocal` declared as a non-static field of a class, and check if the value stored in it holds a strong reference back to the enclosing class instance. If so, flag as a potential memory leak due to reference cycle. 5) For `ExecutorService` submissions, check if the submitted `Runnable`/`Callable` uses `ThreadLocal` and fails to call `remove()` at the end of its `run()`/`call()` method.
